concerns.location: tasks.md:Phase 4: User Story 2 – Summary Report Generation
concerns.text: Contract test T029 and integration test T030 are placed before the implementation tasks (T031‑T037) they are meant to validate, violating the producer‑before‑consumer ordering.

concerns.location: tasks.md:Phase 5: User Story 3 – Export Audit Results
concerns.text: Integration test T039 appears before the implementation tasks (T040‑T044) that generate the artifacts it tests, breaking the required ordering.

concerns.location: tasks.md:Phase 6: User Story 4 – Efficient CI Execution
concerns.text: CI test T045 is listed before the workflow modifications (T046‑T049) it is intended to exercise, causing a consumer‑before‑producer violation.
Task ID T080 is used twice (once in Phase 2 as a reproducibility check and again in Phase X as a success‑criteria verification), creating ambiguous dependencies and breaking unique‑ID ordering.
Task T013 is marked `[P]` but reads all generated output files; if run in parallel with other `[P]` tasks that write those files, it may encounter race conditions, so it is not safely parallel‑safe.

Because contract and integration tests (T029‑T030) precede the core implementation (T031‑T037), the US2 block cannot be shipped and tested independently, violating the independently‑shippable unit requirement.
Integration test T039 precedes its implementation tasks (T040‑T044), preventing US3 from being an independently‑completable story.
CI test T045 precedes the CI‑related implementation tasks (T046‑T050), so US4 cannot be completed and verified in isolation.
